response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
As a retired woman living in Rome, I've spent many years adapting to modern technology. Social media is a big part of my daily routine now, as it allows me to keep up with family and friends across the globe. But lately, I'm finding all that fake news and misinformation quite troubling.

I remember when we first moved to Italy from America back in 1975, everything was new and exciting. Now at age 73, seeing how information can be twisted online reminds me of those confusing times adapting to a different culture.

Being curious has helped me throughout my life learn Italian and understand our rich history. But with social media misinformation about everything from cooking recipes I try experimenting with to family genealogies I research, it's making things complicated.

I've seen how even well-meaning people can spread untruths when they're not careful or informed properly. My moderate neuroticism makes me particularly sensitive to hearing conflicting information that might upset my grandchildren who often visit us here in Rome.

Perhaps the best approach is what I do with my Italian language learning - keep it simple and verify everything myself, even though I only have a lower secondary education. That's how I manage seeing so much fake content on social media these days.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Based on my background as an immigrant woman living in Rome who uses social media daily but has limited formal education, I approach fake content with caution and curiosity. My moderate neuroticism makes me value accuracy to avoid confusion for my family. Drawing from my life experience adapting to new cultures, I believe the key is verification through trusted sources.
